definitions.text: Made up of two matching or complementary elements.

examples: Give me a double serving of mashed potatoes.
Britain's population density used to be double that of China.

definitions.text: Of a family relationship, related on both the maternal and paternal sides of a family.
examples: He's my double cousin as my mother's sister married my father's brother.

definitions.text: Two together; two at a time.
examples: When I met the twins, I thought I was seeing double.
There are only a few beds, so some of the children will have to sleep double for the night.
The old man was bent double under his heavy burden.

definitions.text: Twice the number, amount, size, etc.
A person who resembles and stands in for another person, often for safety purposes
examples: Saddam Hussein was rumored to have many doubles.

definitions.text: A drink with two portions of alcohol.
examples: On second thought, make that a double.
The company doubled their earnings per share over last quarter.

definitions.text: To increase by 100%, to become twice as large in size.
examples: Our earnings have doubled in the last year.

definitions.text: To be the double of; to exceed by twofold; to contain or be worth twice as much as.
